name: Zargham ibn Malik
title: Lion of Taghlib
shortDescription: A brave Shia of Kufa who, after encountering the Imam's army, joined the ranks of the Master of Martyrs (AS) and was martyred.
fullStory:
Dirgham ibn Malik al-Taghlibi was a well-known and brave Shia from Kufa. Living up to his name, which means 'fierce lion,' he possessed exemplary courage.

Initially, Dirgham left the city with the Kufan forces to assess the situation, but as soon as he managed to get close to Imam Hussain's (AS) camp in Karbala, he immediately joined the Imam's companions.

On Ashura, he stood bravely against his former comrades. During the first attack by the Kufan archers on the morning of Ashura, while manfully defending the tents, Dirgham was targeted by arrows and attained martyrdom.

His name is mentioned in the Ziyarat al-Nahiya al-Muqaddasa, reminding us of those who chose the path of truth at the last opportunity.
quote: (Ziyarat al-Nahiya:) Peace be upon Dirgham ibn Malik...
